title: Sensorium Observation v1
description: Admitted local Sensorium observation: a fact about the world normalized by sensorium-core after connector submission or after a successful directive. Rejected and quarantined submissions do not become observation records.

schema.description: Schema tag for the v1 Sensorium contract.

observation/id.description: Opaque observation identifier assigned by sensorium-core; recommended to be ULID-prefixed, e.g. obs:local:01J...

invocation/id.description: Optional connector invocation trace id. Present for reactive observations produced by a directive or explicit connector invocation.

directive/id.description: Optional directive id when this observation was produced as a result of sensorium-directive.v1.

outcome/id.description: Optional directive outcome id linking this observation to the audit-only sensorium-directive-outcome.v1 record.

correlation/id.description: Optional opaque id threading this observation through a higher-level plan or workflow.

connector/id.description: Registered connector module id that produced the submitted observation or executed the directive.

connector/kind.description: Domain-facing connector kind/class label, e.g. OS, public-network-reader, filesystem-watcher.

observed/at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the source event or instrument-level observation. This is the time belonging to the world/source, not the time at which the connector or sensorium-core processed the record.

ingested/at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when sensorium-core admitted the observation and wrote the admitted record. This is not the source event time; use observed/at for the instrument/source timestamp and connector/submitted_at when connector latency matters.

signal/kind.description: Authoritative local signal kind used for query and per-kind local Agora topic derivation. The value forms the suffix of local/sensorium/observations/{signal-kind}. Each '/'-separated segment is a dotted chain of lowercase tokens, which lets connectors carry reverse-DNS vendor prefixes (e.g. com.apple.ios.accelerometer/x-axis) for collision-free namespacing. Bare short names (e.g. release, github-release, filesystem/change) remain valid and represent the community-common namespace. Orbiplex-owned kinds SHOULD use the ai.orbiplex.* prefix per proposal 046.

signal/family.description: Optional generic signal-family identifier declared by the connector alongside signal/kind. Carries the vendor-independent family name (e.g. accelerometer/x-axis, workflow/completed) so consumers can query across providers or subscribe broadly and filter by envelope without maintaining a vendor-to-family mapping. MUST NOT contain dots; the no-dot pattern distinguishes the community-common family namespace from the dotted reverse-DNS namespace used by signal/kind. Source of truth is the connector module report; sensorium-core does not derive this value. No local/sensorium/observations-by-family topic tree exists in v1.

subject/kind.description: Optional kind of observed subject, e.g. github-repository, url, filesystem-path, host, self, environment. Omit only when the observation is genuinely subjectless.

subject/id.description: Optional stable subject identifier within subject/kind. For node-self measurements use a stable self identifier rather than a blank value.

summary.description: Optional human-readable compact summary. Numeric or high-frequency observations MAY omit this field when a textual summary would add no value.

lang.description: BCP 47 language tag for summary text.

class.description: Sensorium-local confidence class; not the emergency C0-C4 credibility scale.

ttl_sec.description: Freshness horizon for read models and time-sensitive consumers. This is not necessarily a deletion deadline for historical/audit storage.

class.description: Local sensitivity class aligned with connector_sensitivity_baseline values.

redaction.description: Connector or Sensorium redaction state label.

kind.description: Source reference kind, e.g. url, file, command, device. Uses the same lowercase slash-separated token convention as signal/kind and local Sensorium topic suffixes, including optional dotted reverse-DNS prefixes for vendor-specific reference kinds.

value.description: Source reference value; may be redacted.

evidence/refs.description: Optional artifact or evidence references using the minimal artifact-lane contract from proposal 045.

policy/hints.description: Connector-supplied policy hints. These are not authoritative.

memarium_admit_hint.description: Connector hint requesting durable Memarium admission. The authoritative decision is admission.memarium_admit.

status.description: sensorium-observation.v1 records are admitted-only; rejected and quarantined submissions do not become observations.

store.description: Required Node-owned generic module store reference and TTL metadata for the admitted observation.

publish_topics.description: Local Agora topics to which this observation was published. v1 topics MUST use the proposal-046 shape local/sensorium/observations/{signal-kind} and are rejected by multi-tenant relays.

connector/submitted_at.description: Optional RFC 3339 timestamp when the connector emitted or submitted the observation candidate to sensorium-core. Used to distinguish source lag (connector/submitted_at - observed/at) from admission lag (ingested/at - connector/submitted_at).

artifactRef.description: Minimal artifact-lane reference. The artifact itself is stored outside this envelope and is addressed by a content or host-owned blob reference.

artifact/id.description: Content-addressed or host-owned artifact identifier. v1 accepts sha256:<base64url-or-hex> and memarium-blob:<opaque-id> forms.

role.description: Role of the artifact in the Sensorium exchange.

media_type.description: Optional media type of the referenced artifact.
